perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will use the Information only for the purpose of collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party and shall not otherwise use or exploit any of the information for its own or any other person’s gain, benefit or purpose and, in particul ar but without limitation, it will not use any of the Information for commercial or competitive purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will use the Details only for the purpose of collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party and shall not otherwise use or exploit any of the information for its own or any other person’s gain, benefit or purpose and, in particul ar but without limitation, it will not use any of the Information for commercial or competitive purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Information' is replaced with 'Details' at the beginning of the sentence. However, the subsequent use of 'Information' in the same sentence makes the meaning unclear. The document fails to define whether "Details" and "Information" are the same. This internal contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement as it's unclear what exactly the recipient is allowed to use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will use the Details only for the purpose of collaboration or trade with the Disclosing Party and shall not otherwise use or exploit any of the information for its own or any other person’s gain, benefit or purpose and, in particul ar but without limitation, it will not use any of the Information for commercial or competitive purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Information' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Details' at the beginning of the sentence, but then continues to use 'Information' later in the same sentence and in the contradicted text, thus creating ambiguity and an internal contradiction. The explanation clarifies that it's unclear whether 'Details' and 'Information' refer to the same thing, making it difficult to enforce the contract, as it is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. The change directly impacts the meaning of the clause and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Information received at any time by it.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will treat and safeguard as private an d confidential and take reasonable steps necessary or desirable to maintain the confidentiality and security of all Data received at any time by it.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced "Information" with "Data". This creates a contradiction because "Information" is already defined in section 1, but Data is not. Furthermore, Section 6 contradicts it as it still uses the word Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will not in any form or manner copy or reproduce or part with possession of any Inform ation or Materials or any other documents or other material or data made available to it or disclosed or procured to be disclosed by the Disclosing Party except as consistent with its obligations under this agreement and in particular (but without limitati on) its obligations under paragraphs 3, 4 and 5 above.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Information" to "Data" creates a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation states that "Information" is already defined in section 1, while "Data" is not. The contradicted text specifically mentions "Information" which is now inconsistent with the changed text. The changed text introduces a Misaligned Terminology, leading to in-text contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: All of the Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the Details shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced "Information" with "Details" at the start of the sentence. This creates ambiguity regarding what remains the property of the Disclosing Party and how it affects the rest of the paragraph where "Information" is still used. This contradiction introduces uncertainty in enforcement, especially since 'Details' is undefined in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All of the Details shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and the disclosure of such Information and the Recipient access to the Materials shall not be deemed to confer upon the Recipien t any rights or licence whatsoever in respect of any part of the Information or Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'Information,' and the changed text replaces it with 'Details' at the beginning of the sentence. The Contradicted Text also contains the term 'Details'. The Perturbation Explanation states that this creates ambiguity because 'Details' is undefined in the agreement, and it contradicts the rest of the paragraph where 'Information' is still used. Since 'Information' is used in the contradicted text, and 'Details' is now undefined, it creates a Misaligned Terminology which the perturbation definitions state can lead to a In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Buyers agree that they will not (collectively or individually and/ or either directly or indirectly) solicit the employment or engagement or attempt to solicit the employment or engagement of the Key Personnel or otherwise employ or engage any of the Vendors Key Personnel (other than in the Target or otherwise following the successful acquisition of the Target) in any capacity whatsoever for 12 months following the date of this letter or the date on w hich the proposed acquisition is aborted (whichever is the latter).
perturbation.changed_text: The Customers agree that they will not (collectively or individually and/ or either directly or indirectly) solicit the employment or engagement or attempt to solicit the employment or engagement of the Key Personnel or otherwise employ or engage any of the Vendors Key Personnel (other than in the Target or otherwise following the successful acquisition of the Target) in any capacity whatsoever for 12 months following the date of this letter or the date on w hich the proposed acquisition is aborted (whichever is the latter).
perturbation.explanation: Replaced "Buyers" with "Customers" in this section. This contradicts the earlier parts of the agreement, specifically where the term "Buyer" was used and defined in the beginning of the file, making it unclear who is bound by this non-solicitation clause. The original intent was for the Buyer, but by changing it here in section 18 to Customer, it creates an in-text contradiction and making its enforcements difficult to pursue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS: (1) The Buyer is proposing t o purchase a company from the Vendor (the “Target”). (2) The Parties wish to exchange information to facilitate this. (3) After an initial investigation period the Parties intend to enter into a letter of intent and, in time, a formal Sale and Purchase Agreement. It is AGREED: 1.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Buyers" to "Customers" introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text defines "Buyer" as a specific party in the agreement. By changing the term to "Customers" in the modified section, the agreement creates ambiguity as to who is bound by the non-solicitation clause, which directly contradicts the original defined term of 'Buyer' and makes its enforcement unclear, fitting the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
